steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the passage. The passage opens two opposing critical camps — one finding fault, one defending — and presents both without resolution. The organizing principle is that contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• The remaining alternative uses for *Master and Commander* makes it one of his most intricate books.": Frames the content as O'Brian's own choice rather than a critics' debate. The passage focuses on what critics argue, not on authorial intent. ✗
  • "Critics have differing views regarding the efficacy of the structures of the novels in the Aubrey Maturin series.": Captures the passage's core: two sides disagreeing over whether the abrupt endings succeed or fail as a structural choice. ✓
  • "Some critics think the Aubrey/Maturin series should have the literary renown of *The Forsyte Saga*, while others disagree.": The Forsyte Saga appears as an analogy for how to read the series (as one long work), not as a benchmark of literary prestige. ✗
  • "Many critics judge the Aubrey/Maturin novels to be remarkably entertaining despite flaws in the novels' structures.": The passage never praises the books for entertainment — that claim is invented. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
